section_miscategorizations.text: Palpation of the right knee reveals tenderness to palpation.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This exact finding (tenderness to palpation in the right knee) is already reported by the patient in the Subjective section and is not clearly distinguished as a separate clinician exam maneuver here; as written, it reads redundantly as the same patient-reported symptom rather than a distinct observed finding.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is icing the knee.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This describes what the patient is currently doing at home (existing self-care), which is part of the subjective history, not a new management action or order.

The template defines O as including procedures already performed; a study that is only planned and not yet ordered or performed is ambiguously placed. It more properly belongs either as an explicit order in P or as a documented order/result in O once done; as written, it is not clearly an active order.
